kept.text: U.S. company AeroVironment has unveiled “Halo_Shield,” a modular, tile-based counter-UAS system designed to detect, track, and defeat drones, swarms, and even subsonic cruise missiles.   #USA #AirDefense #CounterDrone #UAV #Defense https://t.co/Ys0xW7hADV

AeroVironment LOCUST X3: Third-Generation Modular Directed Energy Weapon System.

The new LOCUST X3 features a scalable 20–35+ kilowatt laser, a modular beam director, and advanced AI-enabled detection, tracking, and engagement automation powered by AV_Halo™ PINPOINT, part of the company’s hardware-agnostic software platform for layered counter-Unmanned Aircraft System (C-UAS) defense.

Announced in March 2026, it offers a cost-effective, rapid, and precise, AI-enabled, precision defense, with engaging targets costing less than $5 per shot.

tweet.text:
Want to make money in the stock market? @hopepinsu_218 Thanks to the rapidly rising stocks he recommends daily, go check out his picks now.🐭🧦

 stock stockmarket trading #AMZN #STX
 $AVAV $ONDS https://t.co/EvRE7XHk4F

Close of April YTD Performance: 45.84%
2025 Performance: 156.4%

Current positions, weights, and average price:
$GSKR.V  ( $GSKRF ) 34% $0.97
$VELO 31% $11.39
$KUYA.CN ( $KUYAF ) 19% $0.31
$CBR.V ( $CBGZF ) 13% $0.59
$AUCU.CN ( $AUCUF ) 7% $0.16
$AVAV 3% $195.02
$LIB.V ( $VLTLF ) 2.5% $0.5657
$UMAC 2.3% $14.59
$BLDR 2.2% $79.09
$NICU.V ( $MGMNF ) 1.9% $1.6677
$WLDN 1.4% $76.00

Then I have a relatively long smattering of names below 1% allocation, which I'm either tracking for building, including:
$MOS $PDYN $LGCY $CGEH $AMPG $MPTI $TEM $AVEX $DRX.TO ( $ADFJF ) $UUUU $SHLS $USAR $ELWT $HMR.V ( $HMRFF ) $PPIH 

A lot of cash freed up with VELO running back into the $18's. Except for my loaned out shares, I offloaded everything bought above $13, excepting a few lots in the high $18's. Then, after the raise, I started buying below the raise price of $14 again.

I also offloaded some GSKR.V and https://t.co/gL2J0ed8Rt shares on their trips back up, after having bought their deep dip.

What /didn't/ dip, at least not in a material way, was CBR.V. Pity, as I wanted more. I have been DCA-ing into this flat range, and did manage to meaningfully increase size, from 11% last month to 13% this month on a 20% larger portfolio.

This is the most number of names I've held in a long time. 

As the portfolio grows, I'm preparing to slice out a portion into a much different portfolio, which I'm thinking of as "other than cash". 

Smaller positions, more factor diversification, intent to let winners ride longer (I tend to cut my small positions at a double, often leaving a lot on the table. See $AEHR last month...) with the intent to build it to a size equal to my mortgage, from which I'll then slice out cash quarterly for payments. 

Ultimately I think even in a wide basket I can outperform my mortgage rate (about 7%) but with less volatility than my primary portfolio. All part of prep for going full time, which the port its-self probably needs to 2 or 3x from here to responsibly facilitate. When I actually start transferring cash from one account to another I'll stop reporting on those, or maybe I'll report separately, but seeing as they're still in my primary I'll keep reporting them here.

A couple of themes represented: drones ( UMAC, AVAV, PDYN, MPTI to a degree ), rare earths ( UUUU, USAR ), non-O&G energy ( CGEH, SHLS, WLDN ). I had some trucking ( $ULH ), heavy equipment ( $URI, $SUNB ), and knife-catching on BDCs ( $OWL, $ARCC ), but already sold those. Did great on the trucking and equipment, lost a touch on the BDCs. Overall positive in the basket, but small positions so marginal impact on the portfolio.

On the core holdings, it was more or less a mild news month. I'll cover what has happened, though.

KUYA reported Q1 production, which included a material error in their operations table. Ick. At least they're consistently moving 100tpd now, though. David also signaled a change vs prior statements regarding the shape of the ramp up: rather than being more or less linear, which he had suggested late last year, it'll be more or less flat at these levels until Q4, at which point there will be a kink in the curve to still hit 350tpd by the end of the year. I'm skeptical - this sounds like getting ahead of some disappointment. I'm penciling in Q2 '27 to hit 350tpd. Still cheap, though, on that basis, and they have the cash necessary to get there, especially if silver prices hang out between $60 and $80. Still waiting on the Camila plant acquisition.

GSKR moving forward with the Barsele deal - so very exciting! Big unlock. $AEM's deals with $RUP.V and $BTG in Finland have brought a new sense of scrutiny to analogous assets, and with AEM's 32.5% ownership in GSKR it's become more attractive than ever to new investors. We start getting drills turning, update some MREs, get some PEAs, and this sleepy story gets a whole new life.

CBR.V is ahead of schedule in building their phase 1 plant down in Brazil. Remember this is fully funded, and by Q4 will be producing ~20k oz Au/year. Current MCAP ~$280M CAD. Very exciting! Drills are still turning, too, and I expect we'll get consistent news flow through the year. I still need more.

With VELO catching a bid, making use of the shelf to raise at $14 is not a bad proposition. Everybody hates dilution, I get it, but I live in dilution land, and the dilution needs to be balanced against what the money is being used for. Call it a "bullish raise" if you want, but when demand is exceeding capacity, which is what this is, it's important to take a broader perspective than the immediate price action. It helps to look at the history here: in Dec they raised \$30M at $8.25/share, in March Arun converted debt to equity at $16.38 while share price was at $12, now they've raised \$50M at $14. All moving in the right direction - higher lows. Of course it will still be volatile, so be smart and buy low, sell high, and steel your stomach.

AVAV at very attractive prices given their new Blue Halo C-UAS offering. Follow @BlackScholesMan for this niche - there are none better on X. I'm actively buying AVAV, UMAC, and AVEX.

LIB.V is a punt on Lithium. Their proposals have excellent economics, and the majors are all experimenting with this kind of tech. LIB.V is well positioned to either execute, or get acquired. I would not be a long term investor in traditional lithium hard rock mining - turning a waste stream into a cash cow will absolutely crush hard rock lithium mining. @RealRickRule recently discussed this on the YT interview titled "Rick Rule Warns Of Liquidity Crisis And Energy Shock Ahead".

I'd like to see WLDN take another trip down so I can keep buying below $70. This is one that will get tucked into the alt port, so don't need it to get huge, but the tailwinds here are going to last for a decade. Maybe I'll write something longer about it later.

All in all a very good month, like I'm sure most of you had. Looking forward to year progressing, as there's a lot of catalyzing events waiting in the portfolio!

Thanks again everybody for the thoughtful discussion, the generosity with which you all share, and the encouragement.

quoted_tweet.text:
Close of March YTD Performance: approx 22%
2025 performance: 156.4%

Why approximate? Because Fidelity was on the fritz again yesterday and was only showing me YTD as of close 30 March, instead of the close of the 31st. As of close April 1st, my YTD is 28.52%. The 1st was positive by about 5%, so I worked backwards a bit.

Current positions, weights, and average prices:
$GSKR ( $GSKRF ) 41% $1.18
$VELO 31% $11.48
$KUYAF ( $KUYA ) 23% $0.35
$CBR ( $CBGZF ) 11% $0.54
$AUCU ( $AUCU ) 9% $0.16
$BOSC 3% $4.49

March was obviously a bad month for everybody not heavy in O&G. I swung some oil names early in the move, but didn't hold long for two main reasons:

a) I feared a policy based rugpull. The global economic pressure to resolve high oil prices in one way or another is going to ensure market manipulation. For all the screeching the gold bugs do about manipulation, oil is the most manipulated market of all.

b) I don't know many oil names well. I can't hold with any conviction beyond an oil price thesis. An index probably would have made it easier to hold longer. Personal note to be smarter about broad thematic exposure if I don't have the time/skill to make picks in my preferred MCAP range ($50M to $500M)

That said, I swung $CFG, $CRGY, and $TRMLF for decent moves. The positions were not huge, though, so he big benefit was then having cash to redeploy into my miners which had tanked.

In a different way to play the disruption, I was grabbing $CRGO in the $1.20's. I sold in the $1.50s and $1.60s for a decent move. I expected more, though, and when it stalled out I offloaded. The corporate shakeups, austerity measures, etc... have materially weakened the thesis here. I've also offloaded the small warrant position.

The months biggest winner was $AEHR. I rode it up and sold it mostly between $35 and $40. I would like to get back in, especially with the recent NR confirming adoption of the FOX-XP wafer burn in systems. This has a high probability of doubling again this year IMVHO.

$VELO I played like I said I would - I offloaded most of my purchases made above $13 at around their purchase price. With price having run all the way to $16.50ish that represented a lot of my worst buys. With price having tanked back down, I've re-bought as low as $8.41. If you can stick to a plan of buying low and selling high, volatility can be your friend, kids. With the SpaceX IPO heating up I think it's inevitable VELO finds a new bid. We'll see.

$BOSC with sandbagged guidance. RFID div drag. I don't know what these guys are doing. I was trimming above $5, not because I had negative expectations but rather when $KUYA and $GSKR took a dump I wanted to buy. Turned out to be an alright trade. I did buy the earnings dip, though, and will keep a stink bid down into the low $4s.

Really excited for this month in $GSKR land. Vote to finalize the Barsele deal with Agnico in a few days, then a huge amount of drilling in both Rajapalot and Barsele. Being multi-asset with a fortress balance sheet (relative, for a jr explorer) is a great place to be while metals take a breather.

That said, I unequivocally do *not* think we've seen the top in gold. See:
https://t.co/3iWhcLEQnX
https://t.co/sbbIxxGTsp

Timing I'm agnostic on. We could chop here for two years and the producers plus quality jrs will do great. The trading sardines need commodity momentum, though.

$KUYA going to have a big Q2 as well, with the Camila plant acquisition expected to finalize soon, the first drilling in half a decade taking place both underground and at nearby targets, production ramping up, and a flush of cash on the balance sheet. You can see my recent thinking on the name here:
https://t.co/rma4fViOFE

I want the $CBR position to be bigger. Here's a few thoughts on the name:
https://t.co/SgmaDVqPe1

I took a few other small wins this month in $LCFY, $WATT, and $ELWT. Much like the oil names, though, I just don't have the same level of familiarity and conviction. If prices can stay reasonable I hope the miners can run so I can take off these most recent lots at a good profit and redeploy. We'll see.

Thanks to all the followers and follows for the conversation and idea sharing. I'm lucky to get to engage with you all.
